## Verification Result

### ✅ COMPATIBLE

All server commands used by the shell API type definitions are confirmed
as supported in the official Azure DocumentDB compatibility documentation.

This means every method in `documentdb-shell-api.d.ts` maps to a server
command that DocumentDB supports. Users can safely use all 63 server-backed
methods and 10 shell-only utility methods in their scratchpad files.

### ℹ️ Supported commands not referenced by the shell API

These server commands are supported by DocumentDB but no shell
method in our registry maps to them. Some are expected (admin
commands, session commands, etc.) but others might be candidates
for new shell methods.

- **aborttransaction** (Session Commands)
- **authenticate** (Authentication Commands)
- **collmod** (Administrative Commands)
- **committransaction** (Session Commands)
- **connectionstatus** (Diagnostic Commands)
- **createuser** (User Management Commands)
- **currentop** (Administrative Commands)
- **dropuser** (User Management Commands)
- **enablesharding** (Sharding Commands)

## What This Report Means

This report verifies that the type definitions shipped with the DocumentDB
VS Code extension are consistent with the official Azure DocumentDB
compatibility documentation.

**How to read it:**

- If the **Verification Result** is ✅ COMPATIBLE, every shell method in
  `documentdb-shell-api.d.ts` maps to a server command that DocumentDB
  officially supports. No action is needed.
- If ❌ issues appear under "Commands we reference that are NOT supported",
  it means the upstream DocumentDB documentation has removed support for a
  command that our type definitions still include. The corresponding methods
  should be removed from the `.d.ts` file and the method registry.
- If new commands appear under "Supported commands not referenced by the
  shell API", they are candidates for new shell methods to be added to the
  `.d.ts` file (if they have useful client-side wrapper functions).

**Reference:** https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/documentdb/compatibility-query-language
